response:
Sec. 1  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A'S OFFER BECOMES MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B'S, WE COMPARE THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM BOTH PUBLISHERS.]

Sec. 2  
[LET X REPRESENT THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE FROM THE BOOK, WHICH IS THE VARIABLE WE WILL USE TO COMPARE THE TWO OFFERS.]

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF $3,000 PLUS 5% OF THE SALES REVENUE, SO THEIR TOTAL EARNINGS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS 3000 + 0.05X.]

Sec. 4  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF $1,500 PLUS 10% OF THE SALES REVENUE, SO THEIR TOTAL EARNINGS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 5  
[TO FIND WHEN PUBLISHER A'S EARNINGS EXCEED PUBLISHER B'S, WE SET UP THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X YIELDS THE RANGE OF SALES REVENUE WHERE PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE.]

Sec. 7  
[THE SOLUTION TO THE INEQUALITY INDICATES THAT PUBLISHER A BECOMES MORE BENEFICIAL WHEN THE SALES REVENUE IS ABOVE $15,000.]

Sec. 8  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED RANGE OF $10,000 TO $40,000, PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL FOR SALES REVENUE ABOVE $15,000.]
